prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline (14 atoms) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states (cation and anion) were computed at multiplicity 2 (doublet). Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file; no re-optimization was performed. Fukui indices are condensed via Mulliken population analysis (finite-difference: neutral, N−1 cation, N+1 anion). Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). All three SCF states converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy: true. Charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings reported in the result JSON.

Headline result (most electrophilic site): C-ortho (atom 3), f+ = 2.2975867.
Most nucleophilic site: N (atom 1), f− = 0.20452359.

The ortho/para activation pattern and N as the primary nucleophilic centre are fully consistent with the NH2 group's well-known resonance-donor character in aniline.

CLI: cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --out aniline_fukui_dft.json --stdout path

Output: aniline_fukui_dft.json; plot: agent_call_01_plot.png
